channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Everyone aboard an American Airlines jet that collided with an Army helicopter is feared dead
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — At least 28 bodies were pulled from the icy waters of the Potomac River after an American Airlines jet carrying 60 passengers and four crew members collided with an Army helicopter while landing at Ronald Reagan National Airport near Washington, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, TARA COPP and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: All aboard American Airlines jet that collided with Army helicopter feared dead
item.description: Everyone aboard an American Airlines jet that collided with an Army helicopter while landing at Ronald Reagan National Airport near Washington is feared dead, a fire chief said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Anna Chen, KTLA 5

item.title: Donald Trump talks so much that White House stenographers and everyone else is struggling to keep up
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House stenographers have a problem. Donald Trump is talking so much, the people responsible for transcribing his public remarks are struggling to keep up with all the words.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A food bank netted a huge haul of 13,000 fresh salmon. The catch? The fish were still alive
item.description: AUBURN, N.Y. (AP) — A New York food bank was offered a huge donation of fresh fish this month — but it came with a catch.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Figure skaters among those on flight that collided with Army helicopter
item.description: U.S. Figure Skating released a statement saying athletes, coaches, and family members were on their way home from the National Development Camp held in conjunction with the U.S. Figure Skating Championship in Kansas when the collision happened.
item.dc:creator: Brian Farrell, KREX 5

item.title: CVS gives some customers a way to get into locked cabinets
item.description: CVS said the company is testing the ability to let customers open locked display cabinets using the CVS Health app.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Austin's police chief tells Hispanic leaders she isn't facilitating ICE
item.description: In the meantime, Hispanic organizations are uniting to debunk misinformation, and trying to educate people about their rights.
item.dc:creator: Jala Washington, NewsNation

item.title: Man who killed five people after a neighbor complained about his gunfire gets life in prison
item.description: COLDSPRING, Texas (AP) — A man accused of killing five people in Texas in 2023 after a neighbor complained that he was keeping a baby awake by firing a gun in his yard late at night pleaded guilty Wednesday to murder charges and will serve a life sentence without the possibility of parole, a prosecu
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 30% of Starbucks menu to be cut, CEO says
item.description: Starbucks chairman and CEO Brian Niccol said in a virtual quarterly update message that the company is planning a roughly 30% reduction of the menu, which will affect both food and beverage items.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, Fox 5

item.title: Trump makes moves to expand power, sparking chaos, possible constitutional crisis
item.description: Just a little over a week into his second term, President Donald Trump is taking steps to maximize his power, sparking chaos and what critics contend is a constitutional crisis as he challenges the separation of powers that have defined American government for more than 200 years.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Brittney Griner plans to sign with the Atlanta Dream, ending her 11-year run with Phoenix
item.description: Brittney Griner plans to sign with the Atlanta Dream, marking the end of her 11-year career with the Phoenix Mercury.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Warriors to retire Andre Iguodala's No. 9 jersey
item.description: As Andre Iguodala moves around town in the Bay Area these days, he is regularly stopped by fans eager to praise him for how he carried himself while playing for the Golden State Warriors.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump order aims to end federal support for gender transitions for those under 19
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order aimed at cutting federal support for gender transitions for people under age 19, his latest move to roll back protections for transgender people across the country.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, GEOFF MULVIHILL and HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Weeks after fire ravaged LA's Pacific Palisades, residents return to dig for mementos
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Scores of Los Angeles residents returned Tuesday to wildfire-ravaged Pacific Palisades after officials began letting people drive back into the scorched community, the first opportunity most have had to carefully scour the sites of their former homes.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and EUGENE GARCIA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida congresswoman wants Trump's face to be carved on Mount Rushmore
item.description: The Mount Rushmore National Memorial in South Dakota currently features the 60-foot-tall heads of four U.S. presidents.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, NewsNation

item.title: New Homeland Security secretary joins federal agents to tout immigrant arrests in NYC
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem joined federal agents Tuesday in New York City to announce the arrests of immigrants wanted on criminal charges, including a man accused of kidnapping who was seen on a viral video entering a Colorado apartment with reputed gang members.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Firings, freezes and layoffs: A look at Trump's moves against federal employees and programs
item.description: CHAPIN, S.C. (AP) — Changeover among federal government agencies is normal when a new administration comes to Washington, with presidents regularly dismissing appointees selected by predecessors from opposing political parties.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Largest documented tuberculosis outbreak in US history hits Kansas, officials say
item.description: The Kansas Department of Health and Environment (KDHE) says Kansas is facing the largest documented tuberculosis outbreak in American history.
item.dc:creator: Colter Robinson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump admin's freeze on federal grants, loans
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked a Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans that could total trillions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: These are the most romantic restaurants in the US, according to OpenTable
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Valentine's Day is just around the corner and finding the perfect restaurant to take the one you love can always be challenging.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, KRON4

item.title: Mariano's hosting company-wide hiring event Thursday
item.description: CHICAGO — Mariano's is hosting a company-wide hiring event at all 44 of its locations on Thursday. According to a press release, the supermarket chain will hold in-person interviews from 2 p.m. to 7 p.m for both full-time and part-time jobs.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, WGN 9

item.title: Coca-Cola launches new Orange Cream flavor
item.description: A year after the launch of the short-lived Coca-Cola Spiced, Coke is adding another new flavor to its lineup.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Shooting at Indiana grocery store leaves 3 dead, including the suspect, and 2 officers wounded
item.description: When police “engaged” the shooting suspect outside the store, two officers were wounded and the suspect was fatally shot, according to the Elkhart Police Department.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Egg prices are soaring. Don't expect that to change anytime soon
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — Bird flu is forcing farmers to slaughter millions of chickens a month, pushing U.S. egg prices to more than double their cost in the summer of 2023. And it appears there may be no relief in sight, given the surge in demand as Easter approaches.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, MARK VANCLEAVE and DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rain douses wildfires in Southern California without causing serious mudslides
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The first significant storm of the season brought snow and downpours to Southern California that doused wildfires and caused some ash and mud to flow across streets in the Los Angeles area on Monday.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Senate confirms Scott Bessent as Trump's Treasury secretary
item.description: Scott Bessent on Monday secured confirmation to lead the Department of the Treasury, putting President Trump’s top economic nominee in place ahead of a daunting GOP push to enact Trump's ambitious economic agenda. Senators voted 68-29 on Bessent’s nomination.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, WGN 9

item.title: Jan. 6 rioter recently pardoned by Trump shot, killed by sheriff's deputy
item.description: An Indiana man recently pardoned by President Donald Trump for storming the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, is dead after being shot by a sheriff's deputy while allegedly resisting arrest.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, Fox 5

item.title: Pardoned Jan. 6 rioter killed by Indiana deputy
item.description: JASPER COUNTY, Ind. — An Indiana man recently pardoned by President Donald Trump for storming the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, is dead after being shot by a sheriff's deputy while allegedly resisting arrest.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: White House says Colombia will take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said Sunday night that a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth between the two countries over topics including immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Gabbard's path through Intelligence Committee narrows ahead of key hearing
item.description: Republicans are cautioning that Tulsi Gabbard’s path to confirmation to lead the U.S. intelligence apparatus is narrowing as she seemingly has trouble winning over key GOP senators. Gabbard, along with Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Taylor Swift watches boyfriend Travis Kelce and the Chiefs beat the Bills and get back to Super Bowl
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — You could say that Taylor Swift's championship era has reached its second consecutive season.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mahomes and the Chiefs will face Hurts, Barkley and the Eagles as they seek a Super Bowl three-peat
item.description: Patrick Mahomes, Travis Kelce and the Kansas City Chiefs are going for a three-peat in a Super Bowl rematch. This time, Jalen Hurts and the Philadelphia Eagles have Saquon Barkley. The Eagles and Chiefs will face off for the Lombardi Trophy for the second time in three years on Feb.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Southern California rain helps firefighters but creates risk of flooding and toxic ash runoff
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Rain fell on parts of Southern California on Sunday and the scattered showers were expected to continue overnight, boosting the risk of toxic ash runoff in areas scorched by Los Angeles-area wildfires. Flood watches were in effect through 4 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Percival Everett's 'James' awarded Carnegie Medal for fiction
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — For author Percival Everett, libraries have long been a source of knowledge and discovery and pleasure, even of the forbidden kind.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, Fox 5
